content.content:
1→# Ploy Test Scripts
     2→
     3→This directory contains all test scripts for the Ploy platform. Tests are organized by functional area.
     4→
     5→## Recent Consolidation (September 2024)
     6→
     7→The test suite has been refactored to eliminate redundancy and improve maintainability:
     8→
     9→### Key Changes:
    10→1. **Common Utilities**: Created `common/test-utils.sh` with shared functions
    11→   - Color definitions, logging functions, assertions, and API helpers
    12→   - All scripts now source this file to avoid code duplication
    13→   
    14→2. **Consolidated Tests**:
    15→   - **ARF Storage**: Multiple storage tests now managed by `test-arf-storage-comprehensive.sh`
    16→   - **Version Detection**: Individual language tests consolidated into `test-version-detection-comprehensive.sh`
    17→   - **OpenRewrite**: Renamed from `orw-*` prefix to `openrewrite-*` for consistency
    18→
    19→3. **Archived Scripts**: 
    20→   - Obsolete scripts moved to `archive/` directory
    21→   - Individual version detection scripts (replaced by comprehensive version)
    22→   - Deprecated features (e.g., Heroku certificate integration)
    23→
    24→### Using Common Utilities:
    25→```bash
    26→# All test scripts should now start with:
    27→source "$(dirname "$0")/common/test-utils.sh"
    28→```
    29→
    30→## Test Execution
    31→
    32→Run tests from the VPS environment:
    33→```bash
    34→ssh root@$TARGET_HOST
    35→su - ploy
    36→cd ~/ploy/tests/scripts
    37→./test-<category>.sh
    38→```
    39→
    40→## Test Categories
    41→
    42→### 1. Core Platform Tests
    43→
    44→#### Lane Detection & Build Pipeline
    45→- `test-lane-detection.sh` - Tests automatic lane selection for different app types
    46→- `test-build-pipeline.sh` - Tests build process for each lane (A-F)
    47→- `test-jib-detection.sh` - Tests Java/Scala Jib integration detection
    48→- `test-python-c-extension.sh` - Tests Python C-extension detection
    49→
    50→#### Storage & Artifacts  
    51→- `test-storage-error-handling-unit.sh` - Tests storage error handling
    52→- `test-artifact-integrity.sh` - Tests artifact signing and verification
    53→- `test-image-size-caps.sh` - Tests image size validation
    54→- `test-size-caps-unit.sh` - Unit tests for size validation
    55→
    56→### 2. Application Management Tests
    57→
    58→#### Deployment & Rollback
    59→- `test-app-destroy.sh` - Tests app destruction workflow
    60→- `test-rollback.sh` - Tests rollback functionality
    61→- `test-preview-deployment.sh` - Tests preview environment creation
    62→- `test-ttl-cleanup-unit.sh` - Tests TTL cleanup for preview allocations
    63→
    64→#### Version Detection
    65→- `test-nodejs-version-unit.sh` - Tests Node.js version detection
    66→- `test-nodejs-version-standalone.sh` - Standalone Node.js version tests
    67→- `test-java-version-unit.sh` - Tests Java version detection
    68→- `test-java-version-detection.sh` - Tests Java version extraction
    69→
    70→### 3. Networking & Security Tests
    71→
    72→#### DNS & SSL/TLS
    73→- `test-dns-propagation.sh` - Tests DNS propagation for wildcard domains
    74→- `test-ssl-deployment.sh` - Tests SSL certificate provisioning
    75→- `test-certificate-management.sh` - Tests certificate lifecycle
    76→- `test-acme-integration.sh` - Tests ACME protocol integration
    77→
    78→#### API & Routing
    79→- `test-api-endpoints.sh` - Tests REST API endpoints
    80→- `test-traefik-integration.sh` - Tests Traefik load balancer integration  
    81→- `test-webhook-self-healing.sh` - Tests webhook-based self-healing
    82→
    83→### 4. CLI Tests
    84→
    85→#### Command Implementation
    86→- `test-cli-commands.sh` - Tests all CLI commands
    87→- `test-cli-help.sh` - Tests help messages and documentation
    88→- `test-cli-error-handling.sh` - Tests error handling and validation
    89→
    90→#### Git Integration
    91→- `test-git-integration.sh` - Tests Git repository integration
    92→- `test-git-validation-unit.sh` - Unit tests for Git validation
    93→
    94→### 5. Infrastructure Tests
    95→
    96→#### Health & Monitoring
    97→- `test-health-monitoring.sh` - Tests health check endpoints
    98→- `test-readiness-checks.sh` - Tests readiness probes
    99→- `test-metrics-collection.sh` - Tests metrics and observability
   100→
   101→#### High Availability
   102→- `test-ha-failover.sh` - Tests high availability failover
   103→- `test-nomad-integration.sh` - Tests Nomad job management
   104→- `test-consul-integration.sh` - Tests Consul service discovery
   105→
   106→### 6. Advanced Features Tests
   107→
   108→#### ARF (Automated Remediation Framework)
   109→- `test-arf-circuit-breaker.sh` - Tests circuit breaker system
   110→- `test-arf-parallel-resolution.sh` - Tests parallel error resolution
   111→- `test-arf-pattern-learning.sh` - Tests pattern learning database
   112→- `test-arf-sandbox.sh` - Tests sandbox management
   113→
   114→#### Policy & Compliance
   115→- `test-opa-policies.sh` - Tests OPA policy enforcement
   116→- `test-supply-chain-validation.sh` - Tests supply chain security
   117→- `test-cosign-verification.sh` - Tests container signing
   118→
   119→#### Environment Management
   120→- `test-env-management.sh` - Tests environment variable management
   121→- `test-consul-env-store.sh` - Tests Consul-based env storage
   122→- `test-secrets-handling.sh` - Tests secrets management
   123→
   124→### 7. Integration Tests
   125→
   126→#### End-to-End Workflows
   127→- `test-e2e-deployment.sh` - Full deployment workflow test
   128→- `test-blue-green-deployment.sh` - Blue-green deployment test
   129→- `test-canary-deployment.sh` - Canary deployment test
   130→
   131→#### Platform Integration
   132→- `test-versioning-system.sh` - Tests version management system
   133→- `test-api-deployment.sh` - Tests api deployment
   134→- `test-platform-resilience.sh` - Tests platform resilience
   135→
   136→### 8. Helper Scripts
   137→
   138→#### Utilities
   139→- `test-helpers.sh` - Common test functions and utilities
   140→- `test-upload-helpers-unit.sh` - Unit tests for upload helpers
   141→- `cleanup-test-resources.sh` - Cleanup test resources
   142→
   143→## Test Scenarios Reference
   144→
   145→### Lane/Stack Detection Scenarios
   146→1. Go app with go.mod → Lane A
   147→2. Node app with package.json → Lane B
   148→3. Java app with Gradle+Jib → Lane C/E
   149→4. Scala app with Gradle+Jib → Lane C/E
   150→5. .NET app (.csproj) → Lane C
   151→6. Python app with pyproject → Lane B; with C-extensions → Lane C
   152→7. Presence of fork()/proc → Force Lane C
   153→
   154→### Build Pipeline Scenarios
   155→8. Unikraft A: build tiny image, export health endpoint, boot in QEMU
   156→9. Unikraft B: enable Dropbear when ssh.enabled=true and inject keys
   157→10. OSv Java packer: consume Jib tar → produce image placeholder
   158→11. OCI Kontain: run Java/Scala image under docker runtime=io.kontain
   159→
   160→### Router & Preview Scenarios
   161→12. GET https://<sha>.<app>.ployd.app: when image missing → triggers build; 202 + progress
   162→13. Once healthy → traffic proxy to allocation
   163→14. TTL cleanup for preview allocations
   164→
   165→### CLI Command Scenarios
   166→15. `ploy push` from Git repo: lane-pick, build, sign, deploy
   167→16. `ploy domains add app domain` updates Consul and ingress
   168→17. `ploy certs issue domain` obtains cert via ACME HTTP-01
   169→18. `ploy debug shell app` builds debug variant with SSH
   170→19. `ploy rollback app sha` restores previous release
   171→
   172→## Writing New Tests
   173→
   174→When creating new test scripts:
   175→
   176→1. **Naming Convention**: Use `test-<feature>-<type>.sh` format
   177→   - Types: `unit`, `integration`, `e2e`, or omit for general tests
   178→   
   179→2. **Structure Template**:
   180→```bash
   181→#!/bin/bash
   182→set -e
   183→
   184→# Test description
   185→echo "Testing: <feature description>"
   186→
   187→# Setup
   188→source test-helpers.sh
   189→
   190→# Test cases
   191→test_case_1() {
   192→    echo "Test: <specific test>"
   193→    # Test logic
   194→    assert_equals "expected" "actual"
   195→}
   196→
   197→# Run tests
   198→test_case_1
   199→
   200→# Cleanup
   201→cleanup_test_resources
   202→
   203→echo "✅ All tests passed"
   204→```
   205→
   206→3. **Documentation**: Update this README when adding new test categories
   207→
   208→## Continuous Integration
   209→
   210→Tests are automatically run on:
   211→- Push to main branch
   212→- Pull request creation
   213→- Nightly builds
   214→
   215→See `.github/workflows/` for CI configuration.
   216→
   217→## Test Coverage Goals
   218→
   219→- **Unit Tests**: 80% code coverage
   220→- **Integration Tests**: All API endpoints
   221→- **E2E Tests**: Critical user workflows
   222→- **Performance Tests**: Response time < 200ms
   223→- **Security Tests**: OWASP Top 10 coverage
   224→
   225→## Troubleshooting
   226→
   227→### Common Issues
   228→
   229→1. **Permission Denied**: Ensure running as `ploy` user
   230→2. **Port Already in Use**: Check for running instances
   231→3. **DNS Not Resolving**: Wait for propagation or flush cache
   232→4. **Certificate Errors**: Check ACME rate limits
   233→
   234→### Debug Mode
   235→
   236→Run tests with debug output:
   237→```bash
   238→DEBUG=1 ./test-<name>.sh
   239→```
   240→
   241→### Test Isolation
   242→
   243→Each test should:
   244→- Create its own test data
   245→- Clean up after completion
   246→- Not depend on other tests
   247→- Be idempotent
   248→
   249→## Contributing
   250→
   251→1. Write tests for new features
   252→2. Ensure tests pass locally
   253→3. Update this README
   254→4. Submit PR with test results
   255→
   256→## Comprehensive Test Documentation
   257→
   258→### Test Categories
   259→
   260→### 1. Leader Election and Coordination Tests
   261→
   262→#### 1.1 Single Instance Leader Election
   263→- **Test ID**: LE-001
   264→- **Objective**: Verify single api instance automatically becomes leader
   265→- **Prerequisites**: Consul running, single api instance
   266→- **Steps**:
   267→  1. Start single api instance
   268→  2. Check `/health/coordination` endpoint
   269→  3. Verify `is_leader: true` status
   270→  4. Check Consul KV store for leadership lock
   271→- **Expected Results**: API immediately becomes leader
   272→
   273→#### 1.2 Multi-Instance Leader Election
   274→- **Test ID**: LE-002
   275→- **Objective**: Verify leader election with multiple api instances
   276→- **Prerequisites**: Consul running, ability to start multiple api instances
   277→- **Steps**:
   278→  1. Start first api instance
   279→  2. Verify first instance becomes leader
   280→  3. Start second api instance
   281→  4. Verify second instance becomes follower
   282→  5. Check both instances report correct status
   283→- **Expected Results**: Only one leader, others are followers
   284→
   285→#### 1.3 Leader Failover
   286→- **Test ID**: LE-003
   287→- **Objective**: Verify automatic leader failover on leader failure
   288→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple api instances running
   289→- **Steps**:
   290→  1. Identify current leader instance
   291→  2. Terminate leader process
   292→  3. Monitor follower instances for leadership acquisition
   293→  4. Verify new leader within 30 seconds
   294→  5. Check coordination tasks continue on new leader
   295→- **Expected Results**: New leader elected automatically, coordination tasks resume
   296→
   297→#### 1.4 TTL Cleanup Coordination
   298→- **Test ID**: LE-004
   299→- **Objective**: Verify TTL cleanup only runs on leader
   300→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple api instances, preview jobs with TTL
   301→- **Steps**:
   302→  1. Deploy preview applications with short TTL (5 minutes)
   303→  2. Monitor TTL cleanup logs on all instances
   304→  3. Verify cleanup only occurs on leader instance
   305→  4. Trigger leader failover
   306→  5. Verify new leader takes over cleanup duties
   307→- **Expected Results**: TTL cleanup runs only on leader, transfers to new leader on failover
   308→
   309→### 2. Graceful Shutdown Tests
   310→
   311→#### 2.1 SIGTERM Handling
   312→- **Test ID**: GS-001
   313→- **Objective**: Verify api responds to SIGTERM with graceful shutdown
   314→- **Prerequisites**: API running with active connections
   315→- **Steps**:
   316→  1. Start api and establish HTTP connections
   317→  2. Send SIGTERM signal to api process
   318→  3. Monitor shutdown logs and timing
   319→  4. Verify connections are drained properly
   320→  5. Verify coordination resources are cleaned up
   321→- **Expected Results**: Graceful shutdown within 30 seconds, no connection drops
   322→
   323→#### 2.2 Connection Draining
   324→- **Test ID**: GS-002
   325→- **Objective**: Verify in-flight requests complete before shutdown
   326→- **Prerequisites**: API running
   327→- **Steps**:
   328→  1. Start long-running request (e.g., large file upload)
   329→  2. Send SIGTERM during request processing
   330→  3. Monitor request completion
   331→  4. Verify request completes successfully
   332→  5. Verify server stops after request completion
   333→- **Expected Results**: In-flight requests complete, clean shutdown
   334→
   335→#### 2.3 Resource Cleanup
   336→- **Test ID**: GS-003
   337→- **Objective**: Verify all resources are cleaned up during shutdown
   338→- **Prerequisites**: API running with active coordination
   339→- **Steps**:
   340→  1. Start api as leader with active TTL cleanup
   341→  2. Initiate graceful shutdown
   342→  3. Monitor Consul sessions and locks
   343→  4. Verify coordination sessions are destroyed
   344→  5. Check for resource leaks
   345→- **Expected Results**: All sessions released, no resource leaks
   346→
   347→### 3. Metrics and Monitoring Tests
   348→
   349→#### 3.1 Prometheus Metrics Collection
   350→- **Test ID**: MT-001
   351→- **Objective**: Verify Prometheus metrics are collected and exposed
   352→- **Prerequisites**: API running
   353→- **Steps**:
   354→  1. Access `/metrics` endpoint
   355→  2. Verify Prometheus format output
   356→  3. Check presence of key metrics (requests, uptime, leadership)
   357→  4. Make sample requests and verify counters increment
   358→  5. Check histogram buckets for request duration
   359→- **Expected Results**: Valid Prometheus metrics, counters update correctly
   360→
   361→#### 3.2 Leadership Metrics
   362→- **Test ID**: MT-002
   363→- **Objective**: Verify leadership status metrics are accurate
   364→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple api instances
   365→- **Steps**:
   366→  1. Start multiple api instances
   367→  2. Check `ploy_api_is_leader` metric on all instances
   368→  3. Trigger leadership change
   369→  4. Verify metrics update correctly
   370→  5. Check `ploy_api_leadership_changes_total` counter
   371→- **Expected Results**: Leadership metrics accurate, changes tracked
   372→
   373→#### 3.3 Application Metrics
   374→- **Test ID**: MT-003
   375→- **Objective**: Verify application lifecycle metrics
   376→- **Prerequisites**: Sample applications
   377→- **Steps**:
   378→  1. Deploy application with build tracking
   379→  2. Check build metrics (`ploy_api_builds_total`)
   380→  3. Verify active apps count (`ploy_api_active_apps`)
   381→  4. Monitor build duration histograms
   382→  5. Test failure scenarios and error metrics
   383→- **Expected Results**: Application metrics accurate, build tracking works
   384→
   385→### 4. API Endpoint Tests
   386→
   387→#### 4.1 Health Check Endpoints
   388→- **Test ID**: API-001
   389→- **Objective**: Verify all health check endpoints return correct status
   390→- **Prerequisites**: API running
   391→- **Steps**:
   392→  1. Test `/health` endpoint
   393→  2. Test `/ready` endpoint with dependencies
   394→  3. Test `/live` endpoint
   395→  4. Test `/health/coordination` endpoint
   396→  5. Test `/health/platform-certificates` endpoint
   397→- **Expected Results**: All endpoints return appropriate status codes and data
   398→
   399→#### 4.2 Application Management APIs
   400→- **Test ID**: API-002
   401→- **Objective**: Verify application CRUD operations
   402→- **Prerequisites**: API running, sample app code
   403→- **Steps**:
   404→  1. Create new application via `POST /v1/apps/:app/builds`
   405→  2. List applications via `GET /v1/apps`
   406→  3. Update application configuration
   407→  4. Delete application via `DELETE /v1/apps/:app`
   408→  5. Verify cleanup of associated resources
   409→- **Expected Results**: Full application lifecycle management works
   410→
   411→#### 4.3 Environment Variable APIs
   412→- **Test ID**: API-003
   413→- **Objective**: Verify environment variable management
   414→- **Prerequisites**: API with Consul env store
   415→- **Steps**:
   416→  1. Set environment variables via `POST /v1/apps/:app/env`
   417→  2. List environment variables via `GET /v1/apps/:app/env`
   418→  3. Update variables via `PUT /v1/apps/:app/env`
   419→  4. Delete variables via `DELETE /v1/apps/:app/env`
   420→  5. Verify variables are available during builds
   421→- **Expected Results**: Environment variable management fully functional
   422→
   423→### 5. Storage Integration Tests
   424→
   425→#### 5.1 Artifact Upload/Download
   426→- **Test ID**: ST-001
   427→- **Objective**: Verify artifact storage operations
   428→- **Prerequisites**: SeaweedFS storage configured
   429→- **Steps**:
   430→  1. Upload build artifacts
   431→  2. Verify file integrity with checksums
   432→  3. Download artifacts
   433→  4. Verify downloaded content matches upload
   434→  5. Test error handling for corrupted uploads
   435→- **Expected Results**: Reliable artifact storage with integrity verification
   436→
   437→#### 5.2 Storage Error Handling
   438→- **Test ID**: ST-002
   439→- **Objective**: Verify graceful handling of storage failures
   440→- **Prerequisites**: API with storage configuration
   441→- **Steps**:
   442→  1. Simulate storage service unavailability
   443→  2. Attempt artifact operations
   444→  3. Verify appropriate error messages
   445→  4. Restore storage service
   446→  5. Verify operations resume normally
   447→- **Expected Results**: Graceful error handling, automatic recovery
   448→
   449→### 6. Lane Detection and Building Tests
   450→
   451→#### 6.1 Automatic Lane Detection
   452→- **Test ID**: LD-001
   453→- **Objective**: Verify correct lane detection for different project types
   454→- **Prerequisites**: Sample projects for each lane
   455→- **Steps**:
   456→  1. Test Go project → Lane A/B detection
   457→  2. Test Java project → Lane C detection  
   458→  3. Test Node.js project → Lane B detection
   459→  4. Test containerized app → Lane E detection
   460→  5. Test WASM project → Lane G detection
   461→- **Expected Results**: Correct lane selected for each project type
   462→
   463→#### 6.2 Lane Override
   464→- **Test ID**: LD-002
   465→- **Objective**: Verify manual lane override functionality
   466→- **Prerequisites**: Sample application
   467→- **Steps**:
   468→  1. Deploy with automatic lane selection
   469→  2. Deploy same app with manual lane override
   470→  3. Verify override is respected
   471→  4. Check build logs for lane selection reasoning
   472→- **Expected Results**: Manual lane selection overrides automatic detection
   473→
   474→### 7. Certificate Management Tests
   475→
   476→#### 7.1 ACME Certificate Provisioning
   477→- **Test ID**: CM-001
   478→- **Objective**: Verify automatic certificate provisioning
   479→- **Prerequisites**: Valid domain, DNS configuration
   480→- **Steps**:
   481→  1. Add domain to application
   482→  2. Trigger certificate provisioning
   483→  3. Verify certificate is obtained from Let's Encrypt
   484→  4. Check certificate is stored properly
   485→  5. Verify certificate is used in Traefik configuration
   486→- **Expected Results**: Automatic certificate provisioning and deployment
   487→
   488→#### 7.2 Certificate Renewal
   489→- **Test ID**: CM-002
   490→- **Objective**: Verify automatic certificate renewal
   491→- **Prerequisites**: Certificate near expiration (test cert)
   492→- **Steps**:
   493→  1. Create certificate with short expiration
   494→  2. Wait for renewal trigger
   495→  3. Verify new certificate is obtained
   496→  4. Check old certificate is replaced
   497→  5. Verify no service interruption
   498→- **Expected Results**: Seamless certificate renewal
   499→
   500→### 8. ARF (Automated Remediation Framework) Tests
   501→
   502→#### 8.1 Recipe Execution
   503→- **Test ID**: ARF-001
   504→- **Objective**: Verify ARF recipe execution in sandbox
   505→- **Prerequisites**: ARF system configured, sample Java project
   506→- **Steps**:
   507→  1. Submit transformation request with Java recipe
   508→  2. Monitor sandbox creation and execution
   509→  3. Verify transformation is applied
   510→  4. Check sandbox cleanup after completion
   511→  5. Verify build succeeds with transformed code
   512→- **Expected Results**: Successful code transformation, clean sandbox management
   513→
   514→#### 8.2 Multi-Language Support
   515→- **Test ID**: ARF-002
   516→- **Objective**: Verify ARF works with multiple languages
   517→- **Prerequisites**: Sample projects in different languages
   518→- **Steps**:
   519→  1. Test Java transformation with OpenRewrite
   520→  2. Test Node.js transformation with tree-sitter
   521→  3. Test Python transformation
   522→  4. Test Go transformation
   523→  5. Verify language-specific tooling works
   524→- **Expected Results**: Successful transformations across all supported languages
   525→
   526→### 9. Integration Tests
   527→
   528→#### 9.1 End-to-End Application Deployment
   529→- **Test ID**: INT-001
   530→- **Objective**: Complete application deployment workflow
   531→- **Prerequisites**: VPS environment, sample applications
   532→- **Steps**:
   533→  1. Create application via CLI
   534→  2. Push code changes
   535→  3. Monitor build process through all lanes
   536→  4. Verify deployment to Nomad
   537→  5. Test application accessibility
   538→  6. Update application
   539→  7. Verify rolling update
   540→- **Expected Results**: Complete deployment workflow functions correctly
   541→
   542→#### 9.2 Multi-Instance API Coordination
   543→- **Test ID**: INT-002
   544→- **Objective**: Verify multiple api instances work together
   545→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple api instances, shared Consul/Nomad
   546→- **Steps**:
   547→  1. Deploy multiple api instances
   548→  2. Verify leader election
   549→  3. Submit builds to different instances
   550→  4. Verify coordination of TTL cleanup
   551→  5. Test leader failover during operations
   552→- **Expected Results**: Seamless multi-instance coordination
   553→
   554→### 10. Performance Tests
   555→
   556→#### 10.1 Concurrent Build Handling
   557→- **Test ID**: PERF-001
   558→- **Objective**: Verify api handles concurrent builds
   559→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple sample applications
   560→- **Steps**:
   561→  1. Submit 10 concurrent build requests
   562→  2. Monitor resource utilization
   563→  3. Verify all builds complete successfully
   564→  4. Check for resource leaks
   565→  5. Monitor response times
   566→- **Expected Results**: All builds complete, acceptable performance
   567→
   568→#### 10.2 Leadership Election Performance
   569→- **Test ID**: PERF-002
   570→- **Objective**: Verify leader election doesn't impact performance
   571→- **Prerequisites**: High-load scenario
   572→- **Steps**:
   573→  1. Generate high request load
   574→  2. Trigger leader failover during load
   575→  3. Monitor response times during failover
   576→  4. Verify no request failures
   577→  5. Check recovery time
   578→- **Expected Results**: Minimal performance impact during leader changes
   579→
   580→## ARF Phase 3 Test Scenarios
   581→
   582→### 14. LLM Integration Tests
   583→
   584→#### 14.1 LLM Recipe Generation
   585→- **Test ID**: LLM-001
   586→- **Objective**: Verify LLM can generate valid transformation recipes
   587→- **Prerequisites**: LLM API key configured, OpenAI API accessible
   588→- **Steps**:
   589→  1. Configure LLM integration with valid API key
   590→  2. Create transformation request with Java compilation error
   591→  3. Send request to `/v1/arf/recipes/generate`
   592→  4. Verify generated recipe has valid syntax and structure
   593→  5. Validate confidence score above threshold (0.7)
   594→- **Expected Results**: Valid recipe generated with explanation and confidence score
   595→
   596→#### 14.2 Multi-Language Recipe Generation
   597→- **Test ID**: LLM-002
   598→- **Objective**: Test LLM recipe generation across multiple languages
   599→- **Prerequisites**: Sample projects in Java, JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust
   600→- **Steps**:
   601→  1. For each supported language:
   602→     - Create error context (compilation/runtime error)
   603→     - Generate recipe using LLM
   604→     - Validate recipe syntax for target language
   605→  2. Compare generation quality across languages
   606→- **Expected Results**: 70%+ success rate for all supported languages
   607→
   608→#### 14.3 LLM Caching and Performance
   609→- **Test ID**: LLM-003
   610→- **Objective**: Verify LLM response caching and performance optimization
   611→- **Prerequisites**: Redis cache configured
   612→- **Steps**:
   613→  1. Send identical recipe generation request
   614→  2. Measure response time for first request
   615→  3. Send same request again, verify cache hit
   616→  4. Measure response time for cached request
   617→  5. Verify cache TTL behavior
   618→- **Expected Results**: Cache hit provides <100ms response, 90%+ faster than API call
   619→
   620→#### 14.4 LLM Fallback Handling
   621→- **Test ID**: LLM-004
   622→- **Objective**: Test graceful fallback when LLM unavailable
   623→- **Prerequisites**: Ability to simulate LLM API failures
   624→- **Steps**:
   625→  1. Configure invalid API key or block API access
   626→  2. Attempt recipe generation
   627→  3. Verify fallback to static recipes
   628→  4. Check error logging and metrics
   629→- **Expected Results**: System continues operation with static recipes, error properly logged
   630→
   631→### 15. Multi-Language Transformation Engine Tests
   632→
   633→#### 15.1 Tree-Sitter AST Parsing
   634→- **Test ID**: ML-001
   635→- **Objective**: Verify tree-sitter can parse code for all supported languages
   636→- **Prerequisites**: Tree-sitter parsers installed for Java, JS, Python, Go, Rust
   637→- **Steps**:
   638→  1. For each language, create sample source files
   639→  2. Parse AST using multi-language engine
   640→  3. Verify AST structure contains expected nodes
   641→  4. Check symbol and import extraction
   642→- **Expected Results**: 95%+ parse success rate, accurate symbol/import detection
   643→
   644→#### 15.2 Cross-Language Transformation
   645→- **Test ID**: ML-002
   646→- **Objective**: Test transformations work across different languages
   647→- **Prerequisites**: Sample codebases in multiple languages
   648→- **Steps**:
   649→  1. Apply similar transformation type to different languages
   650→  2. Compare transformation quality and accuracy
   651→  3. Verify language-specific patterns are respected
   652→- **Expected Results**: Consistent transformation behavior across languages
   653→
   654→#### 15.3 WASM Optimization Transformations
   655→- **Test ID**: ML-003
   656→- **Objective**: Verify WASM-specific optimizations work correctly
   657→- **Prerequisites**: WASM sample projects, wasm-opt tool
   658→- **Steps**:
   659→  1. Apply WASM optimization recipe to Rust/Go WASM project
   660→  2. Measure binary size before/after transformation
   661→  3. Verify functionality preserved after optimization
   662→  4. Check optimization level compliance
   663→- **Expected Results**: 20%+ size reduction, functionality preserved
   664→
   665→### 16. Hybrid Transformation Pipeline Tests
   666→
   667→#### 16.1 Sequential Hybrid Transformation
   668→- **Test ID**: HYB-001
   669→- **Objective**: Test OpenRewrite → LLM enhancement pipeline
   670→- **Prerequisites**: Java project with migration needs
   671→- **Steps**:
   672→  1. Execute OpenRewrite recipe first
   673→  2. Enhance result using LLM
   674→  3. Compare confidence scores before/after enhancement
   675→  4. Verify combined result quality
   676→- **Expected Results**: Enhanced result has higher confidence (85%+ vs 70%)
   677→
   678→#### 16.2 Parallel Hybrid Transformation
   679→- **Test ID**: HYB-002
   680→- **Objective**: Test parallel execution of OpenRewrite and LLM
   681→- **Prerequisites**: Complex transformation scenario
   682→- **Steps**:
   683→  1. Execute OpenRewrite and LLM transformations in parallel
   684→  2. Measure total execution time
   685→  3. Compare results from both approaches
   686→  4. Verify best result is selected
   687→- **Expected Results**: Faster than sequential (50%+ time reduction), best result chosen
   688→
   689→#### 16.3 Strategy Selection Accuracy
   690→- **Test ID**: HYB-003
   691→- **Objective**: Verify optimal strategy selection based on complexity
   692→- **Prerequisites**: Various complexity levels of transformation scenarios
   693→- **Steps**:
   694→  1. Create simple, moderate, and complex transformation scenarios
   695→  2. Let system select optimal strategy for each
   696→  3. Verify strategy matches expected approach
   697→  4. Measure success rates for each strategy type
   698→- **Expected Results**: 90%+ correct strategy selection, success rates match predictions
   699→
   700→#### 16.4 Confidence Calibration
   701→- **Test ID**: HYB-004
   702→- **Objective**: Test that confidence scores accurately predict success
   703→- **Prerequisites**: Historical transformation data
   704→- **Steps**:
   705→  1. Execute 100 transformations with confidence scoring
   706→  2. Track actual success vs predicted confidence
   707→  3. Calculate calibration error
   708→  4. Verify confidence thresholds are appropriate
   709→- **Expected Results**: Confidence within 10% of actual success rate
   710→
   711→### 17. Continuous Learning System Tests
   712→
   713→#### 17.1 Pattern Extraction
   714→- **Test ID**: CL-001
   715→- **Objective**: Verify system learns from successful transformation patterns
   716→- **Prerequisites**: PostgreSQL learning database, sample transformation history
   717→- **Steps**:
   718→  1. Record 50+ successful transformations
   719→  2. Run pattern extraction analysis
   720→  3. Verify identified patterns match expected results
   721→  4. Check pattern generalization accuracy
   722→- **Expected Results**: 90%+ pattern recognition accuracy, actionable insights generated
   723→
   724→#### 17.2 Strategy Weight Updates
   725→- **Test ID**: CL-002
   726→- **Objective**: Test automatic strategy weight adjustment based on performance
   727→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple strategy executions with varying success rates
   728→- **Steps**:
   729→  1. Execute transformations using different strategies
   730→  2. Record success/failure rates
   731→  3. Trigger strategy weight updates
   732→  4. Verify weights adjusted in correct direction
   733→- **Expected Results**: 25%+ improvement in strategy selection accuracy over time
   734→
   735→#### 17.3 Recipe Template Generation
   736→- **Test ID**: CL-003
   737→- **Objective**: Verify system can generate reusable recipe templates from patterns
   738→- **Prerequisites**: Identified success patterns from learning system
   739→- **Steps**:
   740→  1. Extract successful transformation pattern
   741→  2. Generate recipe template from pattern
   742→  3. Apply template to new similar scenario
   743→  4. Verify template effectiveness
   744→- **Expected Results**: Generated templates achieve 80%+ success rate on similar scenarios
   745→
   746→#### 17.4 A/B Testing Framework
   747→- **Test ID**: CL-004
   748→- **Objective**: Test A/B testing of recipe variations
   749→- **Prerequisites**: Multiple recipe variants for same transformation type
   750→- **Steps**:
   751→  1. Set up A/B experiment with two recipe variants
   752→  2. Execute transformations using both variants
   753→  3. Collect statistical data on success rates
   754→  4. Verify statistical significance calculation
   755→  5. Graduate winning variant
   756→- **Expected Results**: 95% statistical confidence achieved, winning variant identified
   757→
   758→### 18. Developer Experience Tooling Tests
   759→
   760→#### 18.1 VS Code Extension Functionality
   761→- **Test ID**: DEV-001
   762→- **Objective**: Test VS Code extension for recipe development
   763→- **Prerequisites**: VS Code with ARF extension installed
   764→- **Steps**:
   765→  1. Create new recipe file in VS Code
   766→  2. Verify syntax highlighting and validation
   767→  3. Test recipe preview functionality
   768→  4. Use dry-run mode on sample code
   769→- **Expected Results**: Real-time validation, accurate previews, working dry-run
   770→
   771→#### 18.2 Recipe SDK Usage
   772→- **Test ID**: DEV-002
   773→- **Objective**: Verify Recipe SDK enables easy recipe creation
   774→- **Prerequisites**: Recipe SDK installed
   775→- **Steps**:
   776→  1. Use SDK to create new recipe from template
   777→  2. Add custom transformations using SDK helpers
   778→  3. Test recipe with SDK validation tools
   779→  4. Generate recipe documentation using SDK
   780→- **Expected Results**: Recipe created in <5 minutes, passes all validations
   781→
   782→#### 18.3 Local Development Workflow
   783→- **Test ID**: DEV-003
   784→- **Objective**: Test complete local development workflow
   785→- **Prerequisites**: Local development environment set up
   786→- **Steps**:
   787→  1. Develop new recipe using VS Code extension
   788→  2. Test recipe locally using SDK
   789→  3. Submit recipe to local ARF instance
   790→  4. Verify transformation execution
   791→- **Expected Results**: End-to-end workflow completes successfully
   792→
   793→## ARF Phase 4: Security & Production Hardening Test Scenarios
   794→
   795→### Test 29: Vulnerability Detection and Assessment
   796→
   797→**Purpose**: Test comprehensive vulnerability scanning and assessment capabilities
   798→
   799→**Setup**: 
   800→1. Deploy sample applications with known vulnerabilities
   801→2. Generate SBOMs for each application
   802→3. Configure NVD API access and vulnerability databases
   803→
   804→**Test Steps**:
   805→1. Run security scan on vulnerable Java application:
   806→   ```bash
   807→   ./bin/ploy arf security scan --type sbom --target /path/to/app.jar
   808→   ```
   809→2. Verify vulnerability report contains:
   810→   - Critical vulnerabilities with CVSS scores
   811→   - Affected dependencies with version information
   812→   - Fix recommendations with upgrade paths
   813→   - Risk assessment and prioritization
   814→3. Test different scanning modes:
   815→   - SBOM-based scanning
   816→   - Container image scanning
   817→   - Source code scanning
   818→4. Validate NVD API integration:
   819→   - CVE lookup and enrichment
   820→   - CVSS score calculation
   821→   - Remediation guidance extraction
   822→
   823→**Expected Results**:
   824→- 90%+ accuracy in vulnerability detection
   825→- Complete CVE information retrieval within 30 seconds
   826→- Risk scores match industry standards
   827→- Remediation recommendations are actionable
   828→
   829→### Test 30: Security Remediation Engine
   830→
   831→**Purpose**: Test automated security remediation with OpenRewrite integration
   832→
   833→**Setup**:
   834→1. Prepare codebases with security vulnerabilities
   835→2. Configure OpenRewrite recipes for security fixes
   836→3. Set up sandbox environments for safe testing
   837→
   838→**Test Steps**:
   839→1. Generate remediation recipe for SQL injection vulnerability:
   840→   ```bash
   841→   ./bin/ploy arf remediation generate --cve CVE-2023-1234 --codebase /path/to/vulnerable/app
   842→   ```
   843→2. Apply remediation in sandbox:
   844→   ```bash
   845→   ./bin/ploy arf remediation apply --recipe-id remedy-123 --sandbox
   846→   ```
   847→3. Test different remediation types:
   848→   - Dependency upgrades
   849→   - Code transformations for security fixes
   850→   - Configuration changes
   851→   - Security hardening measures
   852→4. Validate rollback capabilities:
   853→   ```bash
   854→   ./bin/ploy arf remediation rollback --recipe-id remedy-123
   855→   ```
   856→
   857→**Expected Results**:
   858→- 85%+ success rate for automated remediation
   859→- Zero false positives in critical vulnerability fixes
   860→- Complete rollback within 30 seconds
   861→- Comprehensive change validation
   862→
   863→### Test 31: Human-in-the-Loop Workflows
   864→
   865→**Purpose**: Test approval and review workflows for security changes
   866→
   867→**Setup**:
   868→1. Configure stakeholders and approval chains
   869→2. Set up notification systems (email, webhooks)
   870→3. Define security policies and thresholds
   871→
   872→**Test Steps**:
   873→1. Create high-severity remediation requiring approval:
   874→   ```bash
   875→   ./bin/ploy arf workflow create --type approval --priority critical --recipe remedy-456
   876→   ```
   877→2. Test approval process:
   878→   - Notification delivery to stakeholders
   879→   - Approval decision processing
   880→   - Escalation on timeout
   881→   - Multi-level approval chains
   882→3. Test review workflows:
   883→   - Security code reviews
   884→   - Architecture impact assessments
   885→   - Business impact evaluations
   886→4. Validate audit trail:
   887→   ```bash
   888→   ./bin/ploy arf workflow audit --id workflow-789
   889→   ```
   890→
   891→**Expected Results**:
   892→- 100% notification delivery
   893→- Approval decisions processed within 5 seconds
   894→- Complete audit trail for compliance
   895→- Proper escalation handling
   896→
   897→### Test 32: SBOM Security Analysis
   898→
   899→**Purpose**: Test comprehensive SBOM generation and security analysis
   900→
   901→**Setup**:
   902→1. Prepare applications with diverse dependencies
   903→2. Configure syft and grype tools
   904→3. Set up license policy definitions
   905→
   906→**Test Steps**:
   907→1. Generate enhanced SBOM with security metadata:
   908→   ```bash
   909→   ./bin/ploy arf sbom generate --target /path/to/app --format spdx-json --security-analysis
   910→   ```
   911→2. Analyze SBOM for security issues:
   912→   ```bash
   913→   ./bin/ploy arf sbom analyze --sbom-file app.sbom.json --deep-scan
   914→   ```
   915→3. Test different SBOM formats:
   916→   - SPDX JSON/XML
   917→   - CycloneDX JSON/XML
   918→   - Syft native format
   919→4. Validate license compliance checking:
   920→   ```bash
   921→   ./bin/ploy arf sbom compliance --sbom-file app.sbom.json --policy corporate-policy
   922→   ```
   923→
   924→**Expected Results**:
   925→- Complete dependency discovery (95%+ coverage)
   926→- Accurate vulnerability correlation
   927→- License compliance validation
   928→- Security metrics calculation
   929→
   930→### Test 33: Production Performance Monitoring
   931→
   932→**Purpose**: Test real-time performance monitoring and optimization
   933→
   934→**Setup**:
   935→1. Deploy ARF system under load
   936→2. Configure performance monitoring tools
   937→3. Set up alerting thresholds
   938→
   939→**Test Steps**:
   940→1. Monitor performance during high-load operations:
   941→   ```bash
   942→   ./bin/ploy arf monitor start --duration 30m --load-test
   943→   ```
   944→2. Test auto-scaling capabilities:
   945→   - CPU/memory threshold breaches
   946→   - Automatic instance scaling
   947→   - Load distribution
   948→3. Validate circuit breaker functionality:
   949→   ```bash
   950→   ./bin/ploy arf circuit-breaker test --failure-rate 50%
   951→   ```
   952→4. Test rate limiting:
   953→   ```bash
   954→   ./bin/ploy arf rate-limit test --requests 1000 --duration 60s
   955→   ```
   956→
   957→**Expected Results**:
   958→- Sub-100ms API response times under normal load
   959→- Graceful degradation under extreme load
   960→- Circuit breaker activation within 5 seconds
   961→- Rate limiting accuracy within 2%
   962→
   963→### Test 34: Security Compliance Framework
   964→
   965→**Purpose**: Test compliance with security frameworks (OWASP, NIST)
   966→
   967→**Setup**:
   968→1. Configure compliance frameworks
   969→2. Define security baselines
   970→3. Set up reporting templates
   971→
   972→**Test Steps**:
   973→1. Run OWASP compliance assessment:
   974→   ```bash
   975→   ./bin/ploy arf compliance assess --framework owasp --baseline production
   976→   ```
   977→2. Generate NIST cybersecurity framework report:
   978→   ```bash
   979→   ./bin/ploy arf compliance report --framework nist --format pdf --timeframe 30d
   980→   ```
   981→3. Test continuous compliance monitoring:
   982→   - Real-time compliance scoring
   983→   - Deviation alerts
   984→   - Remediation tracking
   985→4. Validate audit evidence collection:
   986→   ```bash
   987→   ./bin/ploy arf compliance evidence --framework owasp --control A06
   988→   ```
   989→
   990→**Expected Results**:
   991→- Complete framework coverage
   992→- Accurate compliance scoring
   993→- Actionable remediation plans
   994→- Audit-ready evidence
   995→
   996→### Test 35: Multi-Tenant Security Isolation
   997→
   998→**Purpose**: Test security isolation in multi-tenant environments
   999→
  1000→**Setup**:
  1001→1. Configure multiple tenant environments
  1002→2. Set up role-based access controls
  1003→3. Define security boundaries
  1004→
  1005→**Test Steps**:
  1006→1. Test tenant isolation:
  1007→   ```bash
  1008→   ./bin/ploy arf security test-isolation --tenant-a app1 --tenant-b app2
  1009→   ```
  1010→2. Validate access controls:
  1011→   - User permission enforcement
  1012→   - Resource access boundaries
  1013→   - Data segregation
  1014→3. Test security event correlation:
  1015→   ```bash
  1016→   ./bin/ploy arf security events --tenant tenant-1 --timeframe 24h
  1017→   ```
  1018→4. Validate encryption in transit and at rest:
  1019→   ```bash
  1020→   ./bin/ploy arf security encryption-test --all-interfaces
  1021→   ```
  1022→
  1023→**Expected Results**:
  1024→- Zero cross-tenant data leakage
  1025→- 100% access control enforcement
  1026→- Complete security event correlation
  1027→- End-to-end encryption validation
  1028→
  1029→### Test 36: Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity
  1030→
  1031→**Purpose**: Test security-focused disaster recovery capabilities
  1032→
  1033→**Setup**:
  1034→1. Configure backup systems
  1035→2. Set up secondary environments
  1036→3. Define recovery procedures
  1037→
  1038→**Test Steps**:
  1039→1. Test security data backup and recovery:
  1040→   ```bash
  1041→   ./bin/ploy arf backup create --include-security-data
  1042→   ./bin/ploy arf backup restore --backup-id backup-123 --validate-integrity
  1043→   ```
  1044→2. Simulate security incident response:
  1045→   - Compromise detection
  1046→   - Incident containment
  1047→   - System recovery
  1048→   - Post-incident analysis
  1049→3. Test security configuration consistency:
  1050→   ```bash
  1051→   ./bin/ploy arf config validate --environment production --against-baseline
  1052→   ```
  1053→
  1054→**Expected Results**:
  1055→- Complete security data recovery
  1056→- Incident response within 15 minutes
  1057→- Configuration drift detection
  1058→- Zero security control degradation
  1059→
  1060→### Test 37: Integration Security Testing
  1061→
  1062→**Purpose**: Test security of all system integrations
  1063→
  1064→**Setup**:
  1065→1. Identify all external integrations
  1066→2. Configure security scanning tools
  1067→3. Set up API security testing
  1068→
  1069→**Test Steps**:
  1070→1. Test API security:
  1071→   ```bash
  1072→   ./bin/ploy arf security api-test --endpoint /v1/arf/security --auth-test
  1073→   ```
  1074→2. Validate webhook security:
  1075→   - Signature verification
  1076→   - Payload validation
  1077→   - Rate limiting
  1078→3. Test third-party integration security:
  1079→   - NVD API security
  1080→   - Git repository access
  1081→   - Container registry security
  1082→4. Database security testing:
  1083→   ```bash
  1084→   ./bin/ploy arf security db-test --connection-security --injection-test
  1085→   ```
  1086→
  1087→**Expected Results**:
  1088→- All API endpoints properly secured
  1089→- Webhook authenticity verified
  1090→- Third-party connections encrypted
  1091→- Database injection prevention
  1092→
  1093→## ARF Deployment Integration Tests
  1094→
  1095→### ARF-DI-001: ARF Deployment Integration API Verification
  1096→- **Test ID**: ARF-DI-001
  1097→- **Objective**: Verify existing ARF API endpoints function correctly with new DeploymentSandboxManager naming
  1098→- **Prerequisites**: API running with updated ARF deployment integration
  1099→- **Steps**:
  1100→  1. Check ARF health endpoint: `GET /v1/arf/health`
  1101→  2. Test recipe listing: `GET /v1/arf/recipes`
  1102→  3. Test sandbox management: `POST /v1/arf/sandboxes`
  1103→  4. Verify benchmark endpoints: `GET /v1/arf/benchmark/status`
  1104→  5. Test transformation endpoints with deployment integration
  1105→- **Expected Results**: All API endpoints respond correctly with new naming conventions
  1106→
  1107→### ARF-DI-002: Multi-Stage Benchmark Pipeline Testing
  1108→- **Test ID**: ARF-DI-002
  1109→- **Objective**: Verify new multi-stage benchmark pipeline: transformation → deployment → testing → cleanup
  1110→- **Prerequisites**: ARF benchmark system configured with DeploymentSandboxManager
  1111→- **Steps**:
  1112→  1. Create benchmark configuration with Java test repository
  1113→  2. Execute benchmark via API: `POST /v1/arf/benchmark/run`
  1114→  3. Monitor all pipeline stages: transformation, deployment, application_testing, error_detection, cleanup
  1115→  4. Verify deployment stage creates actual applications using api APIs
  1116→  5. Verify application_testing stage validates HTTP endpoints
  1117→  6. Verify cleanup stage removes deployed applications
  1118→- **Expected Results**: Complete pipeline executes successfully with real application deployment
  1119→
  1120→### ARF-DI-003: DeploymentSandboxManager Functionality Testing
  1121→- **Test ID**: ARF-DI-003
  1122→- **Objective**: Verify DeploymentSandboxManager integration with core deployment system
  1123→- **Prerequisites**: API with deployment integration enabled
  1124→- **Steps**:
  1125→  1. Test sandbox creation via DeploymentSandboxManager
  1126→  2. Verify application deployment using api build APIs
  1127→  3. Test sandbox listing and metadata retrieval
  1128→  4. Verify sandbox cleanup and application removal
  1129→  5. Test error handling for deployment failures
  1130→- **Expected Results**: DeploymentSandboxManager successfully manages application lifecycle
  1131→
  1132→### ARF-DI-004: Application HTTP Endpoint Validation
  1133→- **Test ID**: ARF-DI-004
  1134→- **Objective**: Verify transformed applications are deployed and accessible via HTTP
  1135→- **Prerequisites**: ARF benchmark with deployment integration
  1136→- **Steps**:
  1137→  1. Execute transformation benchmark on Java test application
  1138→  2. Verify deployed application responds to health check: `/healthz`
  1139→  3. Test root endpoint accessibility and response codes
  1140→  4. Validate performance metrics collection from HTTP tests
  1141→  5. Verify error detection when endpoints fail
  1142→- **Expected Results**: Deployed applications respond to HTTP requests with proper error handling
  1143→
  1144→### ARF-DI-005: Error Detection & Log Analysis Functionality
  1145→- **Test ID**: ARF-DI-005
  1146→- **Objective**: Verify comprehensive error detection and deployment log analysis
  1147→- **Prerequisites**: ARF system with error injection capabilities
  1148→- **Steps**:
  1149→  1. Execute benchmark with intentional build errors
  1150→  2. Verify deployment log parsing and error categorization
  1151→  3. Test build system validation error detection
  1152→  4. Verify configuration error analysis functionality
  1153→  5. Test dependency conflict detection
  1154→- **Expected Results**: Error detection system accurately identifies and categorizes failures
  1155→
  1156→### ARF-DI-006: End-to-End Deployment Integration Workflow
  1157→- **Test ID**: ARF-DI-006
  1158→- **Objective**: Validate complete ARF workflow with real application deployment
  1159→- **Prerequisites**: Full ARF system with deployment integration
  1160→- **Steps**:
  1161→  1. Clone test Java repository for transformation
  1162→  2. Apply OpenRewrite transformation recipe
  1163→  3. Deploy transformed application via DeploymentSandboxManager
  1164→  4. Execute HTTP endpoint testing on deployed application
  1165→  5. Collect comprehensive metrics and error analysis
  1166→  6. Verify automatic cleanup of all resources
  1167→- **Expected Results**: Complete end-to-end workflow succeeds with real deployment testing
  1168→
  1169→## Success Criteria
  1170→
  1171→### Reliability
  1172→- 99.9% uptime with automatic failover
  1173→- Zero data loss during failovers
  1174→- Graceful handling of all error conditions
  1175→
  1176→### Performance  
  1177→- Sub-100ms API response times
  1178→- Leader failover in <30 seconds
  1179→- Concurrent build handling without degradation
  1180→
  1181→### Operational
  1182→- Complete observability through metrics
  1183→- Automated recovery from common failures
  1184→- Clean resource management
  1185→
  1186→---
  1187→
  1188→For more information, see the main [Ploy documentation](../docs/).

content.text: This is a general testing documentation file that doesn't need updating specifically for the healing branch types implementation. Now let me run goimports to ensure the Go files are properly formatted as required by the global configuration:

input.description: Run goimports on transflow module files

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me provide a summary of the documentation updates completed.

## Documentation Update Summary

**Repository Structure Detected**: Mono-repo with services - Single repository with internal/ directory containing service modules

**Services Updated**:

### 1. internal/cli/transflow - Main transflow CLI module
**File**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/internal/cli/transflow/CLAUDE.md`

**Key Updates Made**:
- Updated ProductionBranchRunner interface reference location from types.go to fanout_orchestrator.go:17-27 with new GetTargetRepo() method
- Enhanced runner.go documentation to include GetTargetRepo() method implementation at lines 189-191
- Added comprehensive test coverage documentation for job_submission_test.go covering all three healing branch types (1400+ lines)  
- Added MockProductionBranchRunner implementation documentation with complete interface support
- Updated fanout orchestrator line references to reflect complete 420-line implementation
- Enhanced branch type descriptions with complete implementation details:
  - Human-step: Git MR workflow with commit polling and build validation
  - LLM-exec: HCL job submission with diff.patch artifact processing  
  - ORW-gen: Recipe generation with comprehensive template substitution
- Updated "Provides" section to highlight complete branch type system and ProductionBranchRunner interface implementation
